Add TailwindCSS for easy customization of Utility CSS ### Fixes - Optimize Paddings/Margins - Optimize RTL - Optimize Styling for Layouts ## 1.0.0 (2019-08-22) ### Features - Initial release --- designflow-tracking\README.md --- # Designflow Tracking Tracking backend service for Designflow production and licensing flows. This API serves tracking-related endpoints and shares the same Node/Express + PostgreSQL architecture as other platform services. ## Stack - Node.js (CommonJS) - Express - PostgreSQL + Sequelize - JWT + upload/media dependencies ## Project Layout - `index.js`: server setup - `routes/lib.router.js`: tracking routes - `routes/lic.router.js`: licensing routes - `routes/health.router.js`: health endpoint - `models/`: database models - `controllers/`, `helpers/`: business logic ## Environment Setup This repo loads `.env. 0, < 1,000,000). | | `datepicker/` | AG Grid cell editor for date fields. | | `layout-dropdown/` | Toolbar component for saving/loading named grid view layouts. | | `newVendor/` | Dialog to create a new vendor and assign it to an RFQ item. | | `vendor/` | "Select Factories" dialog — attach one or more factories to an RFQ item. Factories already on the item are shown red + unclickable so staff can't add a duplicate (see "Detail grid" below). | Grid column definitions live in **`src/app/helpers/ag-grid/rfq-grid/rfq.grid.config.ts`**, not in this folder. --- ## Key concept: `getMasterFiledName(colId)` Every piece of code that reads or writes to a row's data must go through `getMasterFiledName(colId)` to get the actual storage field name. This function iterates `this.colHeaders` (the column headers loaded from the backend) and returns the `field` value for a given `colId`. - `colId` = stable lowercase-underscore identifier (e.g. `"fob_cost"`, `"cbm_pc"`) - `field` = the backend storage key, which may contain spaces or special chars (e.g. `"FOB Cost"`, `"CBM/pc"`) **Note:** The function name has a typo (`Filed` instead of `Field`). This is preserved intentionally — see AGENTS.md §11. In `equation.ts`, the same function takes `colHeaders` as a second parameter: `getMasterFiledName(colId, colHeaders)`. --- ## Calculation pipeline **For all pricing formulas, INCOTERM cost bases, deduction logic, and scenarios see [`docs/rfq-math.md`](../../../../docs/rfq-math.md). Only read that file when working on math — skip it otherwise.** ### Recalculation trigger chain When a sourcing input is edited, the valueSetter in `rfq.component.ts` calls: ``` calculateLandedCost() ← only if fob_cost / agent / duty / freight changed recalculate_Lic_Gen() → setGenFobMargin → gen_fob_sell_price → setGenMddpMargin → gen_mddp_sell_price → setGenPoeMargin → gen_poe_sell_price → setGenWhseMargin → gen_whse_sell_price → setLicFobMargin → lic_fob_sell_price → setLicMddpMargin → lic_mddp_sell_price → setLicPoeMargin → lic_poe_sell_price → setLicWhseMargin → lic_whse_sell_price ``` All 8 setters run on every triggering edit to keep all columns in sync. Sell-price setters are only triggered when the user directly edits a sell-price cell. --- ## Price for Sales snapshot tooltips The 8 `*_price_sales` columns are manually entered (the price sent to the sales team). A price-for-sales value is only meaningful relative to the **Duty Rate %** and **Container Price** at the time it was set — both of which drift. So when one of these cells is edited, `onCellValueChanged` calls `capturePriceSalesSnapshot()` (in `helpers/price-sales-snapshot.ts`), which records `{ duty_rate, duty_dollar, container_price, at }` into `params.data.price_sales_snapshots[colId]`. Clearing the cell removes that entry. The snapshot is persisted by the existing `updateRfqItem(params.data)` call (backend column `rfqItem_price_sales_snapshots`, TEXT/JSON). The columns render through `priceSalesSnapshotRenderer`, which sets a native `title` tooltip from the stored snapshot so it always shows on hover. Rows with no recorded snapshot (legacy) show no tooltip. See AGENTS.md §11 for why a native tooltip is used here instead of the shared `customTooltip`. --- ## AG Grid patterns specific to this module ### Async valueSetters Every valueSetter that calls `recalculate_Lic_Gen` is `async`. AG Grid v35 awaits the returned Promise — a rejection cancels the cell edit and reverts the value. Always: - Return `true` on success - Return `false` and show a snackbar on validation failure - Do not let any exception escape (the try-catch in `recalculate_Lic_Gen` itself covers margin errors, but the outer setter must also be safe) ### Column identity: never hardcode field names ```typescript // Correct params.data[this.getMasterFiledName('cbm_pc')] // Wrong params.data['CBM/pc'] ``` ### Duty rate: always use optional chaining The duty rate can be stored as `{ title: "7.5" }` (object from autocomplete) or as a raw numeric string. Always: ```typescript const dutyRateRaw = data[this.getMasterFiledName('duty_rate')]; const duty_rate = dutyRateRaw?.title ?? dutyRateRaw ?? 0; ``` ### cellDataType: false on numeric string columns Any column that uses `numericCellEditor` AND whose backend data is stored as strings **must** have `cellDataType: false` in its runtime setup block. Without it, AG Grid v35 infers the column type as `'text'` and issues warning `#135`, silently reverting the edit before the valueSetter is called. ```typescript } else if (colHeaders[i].colId == "cbm_pc") { colHeaders[i].cellDataType = false; // ← prevents warning #135 colHeaders[i]['cellEditor'] = 'numericCellEditor'; colHeaders[i].valueSetter = async (params) => { ... }; } ``` Do NOT use `cellDataType: 'number'` — that applies AG Grid's built-in number formatter and shows "invalid number" for string-valued cells loaded from the backend. The `numericCellEditor` already enforces number-only input via `getValue()` returning `null` for any non-parseable value, so `cellDataType: false` does not open the door to bad data. ### AG Grid deprecated APIs — what NOT to use | Removed | Replacement | Removed in | |---|---|---| | `params.column.userProvidedColDef` | `params.column.getColDef()` | AG Grid v35 | | `params.columnApi` | `params.api` | AG Grid v31 | | `getRowNodeId` | `getRowId` | AG Grid v28 | | `observable.toPromise()` | `firstValueFrom(observable)` | RxJS 7 (deprecated) | --- ## Detail grid (vendor quotes) Each RFQ item row expands to show vendor quotes in a detail grid managed by `detail-cell-renderer.component.ts`. The detail grid: - Loads its own column definitions from the backend (`getGridHeader({ grid_id: 'rfq_detail' })`) - Has its own `onCellValueChanged` that calls `updateVendor(data)` on every change - Uses `addDetailGridInfo` / `removeDetailGridInfo` for master-detail grid registration - The `price_code` column change in the detail grid sets `quote_accepted = true` on the vendor row - Default-sorts by factory price ascending — see "Default sort" below ### Default sort: lowest price first, unpriced/declined last `configureDetailColumnHeaders()` calls `applyDefaultPriceSort(colHeaders)` after building the column defs. This makes the **price column the primary default sort** (`sort: 'asc'`, `sortIndex: 0`); the `name` column stays as the secondary alphabetical tiebreaker (`sortIndex: 1`). A custom comparator on the price column ranks rows so that any quote with no usable price (blank / zero / negative `Price`) or where the factory **declined to quote** (`req_status == 1`, set by the "pass" button in `rfq-button.component.ts`) is sunk to the bottom regardless of value; real quotes are ordered lowest-price-first above them. The comparator reads `node.data.Price` directly (via `isUnpricedOrDeclined()` / `toPrice()`), so it does not depend on the column's `colId`, `field`, or `valueGetter`. **Why `applyDefaultPriceSort` searches for the colId instead of hardcoding it:** the backend `rfq_detail` layout serves the visible price column under colId **`price`** (legacy), while the config file `rfq.detail.grid.config.ts` uses `quote_price`. The helper targets whichever of the two the layout actually provides, preferring the visible one. The first attempt (`e8ba0551`) hardcoded `quote_price` and silently did nothing because the live column is `price`; fixed in `b241ce79`. See `AGENTS.md §11 → "Detail-grid default sort targets the live price colId, not a hardcoded one"`. ### Vendor quote rows and the duplicate-factory guard Each item's quotes live on the master row's **`Vendors`** array (capital V — Sequelize association name); each element is `{ Vendor: { id, ... }, Price, Note, Status, quote_id, ... }`. The same `Vendor.id` may appear more than once: - **Allowed (factory side):** the detail sub-grid "Add Quote" button lets a factory submit a second quote line for the item, as long as a note explains why the price differs. This path is intentionally **unguarded**. - **Blocked (staff side):** the "Add Factory" → "Select Factories" dialog (`vendor/`) must not let sourcing/production/admin add a factory that is already on the item. `onBtnNewVendor()` builds `existingVendorIds` from `node.data.Vendors.map(v => v?.Vendor?.id)` and passes them as `DialogData.vendors`; the dialog renders those factories red and unclickable. The guard is **UI-only** — there is no DB unique constraint or backend dedupe on `(RFQ item, vendor)`, and it depends on the exact capital-`V` `Vendors` casing. See `AGENTS.md §11 → "Same factory twice on one RFQ item"`. ### Re-quote a stale price (orange flag + Keep Price) When a factory's price is old, internal Sourcing/admin can right-click the detail-grid price cell → **"Request new quote"** (`getContextMenuItems`, gated to `admin`/`sourcing_manager`/`production`). That calls `itemService.requestRequote({ quote_id, priceCode })`, which sets `RFQVendor.requote_requested = true` and emails the factory. The flag drives, in **both** the staff and vendor views: - an **orange** price cell — `cellClassRules: { 'requote-requested-cell': … }`, added to **both** the `quote_price` and legacy `price` colId branches (the live layout may serve either — same reason as the default-sort helper above); - a **bilingual tooltip** `报价 - Quoted ` via `tooltipValueGetter`, using the original `quote_date` (which `requestRequote` leaves untouched). The vendor clears it two ways: typing a **new price** (the `quote_price` valueSetter sets `requote_requested = false` locally; backend clears it on save) or clicking the **保留价格 - Keep Price** button (`rfq-button.component.ts` `onKeepPrice()` → `itemService.keepPrice({ quote_id })`, which clears the flag and refreshes `quote_date` to today). Vendor-facing strings are inline `中文 - English` (no i18n framework — see `AGENTS.md §11`). The backend guards both mutating endpoints with `vendorMayModifyQuote` so a vendor can't act on another factory's quote via `quote_id`. --- ## Window globals (known tech debt) `window['itemService']` references in toolbar callbacks were resolved in commit `40b3ac9` — the service is now captured at closure-creation time in `getContextMenuItems()`. Remaining `window` globals in `rfq.component.ts` (`window['router']`, `window['gridApi']`, `window['base64Images']`, `window['togglePageToolbar']`) are used for inter-callback communication in contexts where Angular DI is not available. **Do not add new `window['...']` references.**
